subject: [PATCH v2 05/14] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Mike Rapoport pointed out a minor inconsistency in the naming convention for MM_CP_UFFD_RWP flags, suggesting that 'do rwp' and 'Resolve rwp' should use the same case as other flags.

Reviewer noted that the uffd_wp and uffd_rwp flags are always ORed together, suggesting they be folded into a single flag to simplify code
reviews.raw_body:
Nit: any reason except copy/paset to use different case in "do rwp" and
"Resolve rwp"? ;-)

---

It looks like uffd_wp* are always ORed with uffd_rwp, we could fold this to
e.g.

	bool uffd_prot = cp_flags & (MM_CP_UFFD_WP | MM_CP_UFFD_RWP);

2026-05-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new change_protection() flag, MM_CP_UFFD_RWP, which is used by userfaultfd (UFFD) to implement read-write protection. The flag installs a PAGE_NONE PTE with the uffd bit set, distinguishing it from other types of faults. This allows UFFD to protect pages from being accessed without its permission. The patch also introduces a new resolve flag, MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE, which restores the original page protection and clears the uffd bit. The changes are applied to various memory management functions, including change_pte_range(), change_huge_pmd(), and hugetlb_change_protection(). This patch is part of a larger series that aims to improve UFFD's functionality and security.
